profile.name: Joseph Bailey
profile.tagline: coterm b.s. physics + m.s. cs (ai) @ stanford · ml for fundamental physics
profile.summary: I'm a coterminal B.S. Physics and M.S. Computer Science (AI track) student at Stanford, graduating June 2027. I work on machine learning for fundamental physics—making AI a rigorous, calibrated instrument for science, with honest uncertainties. I'm drawn to compact objects, statistical inference, and secure systems. Off the clock I run the 400-meter hurdles for Stanford Track & Field and play jazz and classical piano.

scenes.label: The Milky Way
scenes.comparison: A galaxy-scale establishing shot: tens of thousands of light years compressed into one navigable frame.
scenes.meaning: This is the universe-first thesis: start with wonder, then turn the lights toward evidence.
scenes.route: Scan for compact binaries, particle-physics beacons, and the amber route home.

scenes.route: Open the ephemeris, focus Earth, and continue inward.

scenes.comparison: A sparse desk-scale field station where objects point to real work.

research.title: Neutrino cross-section unfolding with machine learning
research.description: Implementing and evaluating OmniFold, an unbinned ML unfolding framework, on MINERvA neutrino-scattering data. I benchmark it against iterative Bayesian unfolding through closure tests, generator stress tests, bootstrap resampling, and bias–variance analysis, asking whether high-dimensional unbinned unfolding can reduce structural model bias in the few-GeV regime.
research.meta: Prof. Benjamin Nachman, Stanford Physics · 2026–present · 2026 Stanford Major Grant

research.title: Collider machine learning — SLAC
research.description: On a four-person team proposing a γγ collider, using Delphes, ROOT, and Unix HPC to simulate and analyze terabytes of collider data and applying ML to study pileup effects on di-Higgs detection.

research.title: Gaussian-process bump hunting — Jefferson Lab
research.description: Used Gaussian-process regression to search for excesses from the hypothetical X17 particle in collider data and presented the work at the APS Far West Section meeting at UC Santa Cruz.

research.title: AM CVn time-series photometry
research.description: Reduced 140 V-band CCD images and performed AAVSO differential photometry on 138 retained exposures, recovering variability consistent with the known 1051-second positive-superhump family.

research.title: Interferometry and suspension systems — Caltech LIGO Lab
research.description: Supported simulation, testing, and preparation of suspension elements, and conducted laser-beam interferometry experiments with Python analysis.

experience.title: Product Management Intern — WisdomTree Digital Movement
experience.description: Supporting product management, market research, internal documentation, and product-development discussions for digital-asset financial products, connecting technical work in blockchain with financial markets and client-focused strategy.
experience.meta: Digital Assets Team · New York, NY · June 2026–present

experience.title: Engineering Intern — Polytec
experience.description: Designed Python, MATLAB, and Octave statistical tools for customers analyzing vibration data, and modeled and 3D-printed laboratory tools to streamline operations.

projects.title: This website
projects.description: A Powers-of-Ten-style zoom from the Milky Way to my desk, built with TypeScript and Three.js with a real star catalog, live Earth terminator, and animated AM CVn binary.
projects.challenge: Present research, engineering, and personality without flattening them into a conventional project grid.
projects.contribution: Designed and built the universe-scale navigation, BaileyOS desktop, accessible quick portfolio, scene loading, and responsive interaction system.
projects.outcome: One portfolio with an immersive WebGL route and a fast conventional route, sharing the same structured content and downloadable evidence.

projects.title: MINERvA-OmniFold
projects.description: An implementation and evaluation pipeline for unbinned machine-learning unfolding of neutrino cross-sections.
projects.challenge: Test whether high-dimensional unbinned unfolding can reduce structural model bias in few-GeV neutrino measurements.
projects.contribution: Implemented the OmniFold workflow and an evaluation program spanning closure tests, generator stress tests, bootstrap resampling, and bias–variance analysis.
projects.outcome: A reproducible research pipeline and paper that compare machine-learning unfolding with iterative Bayesian unfolding under controlled uncertainty tests.

projects.title: SPLoRA — parameter-efficient GPT-2
projects.description: LoRA fine-tuning of GPT-2 for paraphrase detection and Shakespearean sonnet generation: 0.888 development accuracy while training about 1% of parameters, with 41.94 chrF.
projects.challenge: Adapt GPT-2 to discriminative and generative tasks without the cost of full-model fine-tuning.
projects.contribution: Built LoRA-based training and evaluation paths for paraphrase detection and Shakespearean sonnet generation.
projects.outcome: Reached 0.888 development accuracy while training about 1% of parameters, with 41.94 chrF on generation.

projects.title: LoRD — video person re-identification
projects.description: Video person re-identification with DINOv2/v3 vision backbones, LoRA adaptation, BNNeck, and temporal attention, evaluated on the MARS benchmark.

projects.title: Soccer action prediction with graph neural networks
projects.description: Modeled soccer possessions as heterogeneous graphs using GraphSAGE and a Relational Graph Transformer with PyG and RelBench to predict the best next action.
projects.challenge: Represent the relational and temporal structure of a soccer possession well enough to predict the best next action.
projects.contribution: Modeled possessions as heterogeneous graphs and evaluated GraphSAGE and a Relational Graph Transformer.
projects.outcome: Produced a reproducible graph-learning project with public code and a technical explanation of the modeling approach.

projects.title: Predicting professional League of Legends matches
projects.description: Machine learning over draft, economy, and objective data to predict the outcomes of professional League of Legends matches, with calibrated win-probability models benchmarked against baselines.

projects.title: Systems and security
projects.description: Built a Solidity and Foundry 2-of-3 multisig Ethereum wallet, an end-to-end encrypted chat client implementing the Double Ratchet, and Pintos OS enhancements spanning scheduling, system calls, and virtual memory.

amCvn.title: AM CVn — time-series photometry
paragraphs: AM Canum Venaticorum is an ultracompact binary: a white dwarf accreting helium-rich material from a degenerate companion in an orbit of just 5–65 minutes. Systems like it are among the strongest known sources of millihertz gravitational waves—verification binaries the LISA mission is guaranteed to detect.

For Physics 100: Introduction to Observational Astrophysics (Spring 2026), I ran physically motivated time-series photometry on AM CVn. I reduced 140 V-band CCD images with bias, dark, and star-masked flat-field calibration, then performed AAVSO-sequence differential photometry on the 138 retained exposures.

The light curve recovered variability consistent with the known 1051-second positive-superhump or harmonic family, and I assessed whether the 111-minute observing baseline could meaningfully refine published period constraints.
